statements.sql: UPDATE tgt SET tgt.EnglishProductName = src.ProductName, tgt.LastSeenDate = GETDATE() FROM silver.UpdateJoinTarget AS tgt INNER JOIN bronze.Product AS src ON tgt.ProductAlternateKey = CAST(src.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25))

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT tgt.ProductAlternateKey, src.ProductName AS EnglishProductName, GETDATE() AS LastSeenDate FROM silver.UpdateJoinTarget AS tgt INNER JOIN bronze.Product AS src ON tgt.ProductAlternateKey = CAST(src.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25))
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import_bronze_product AS ( SELECT * FROM bronze.Product ), logical_update_join AS ( SELECT tgt.ProductAlternateKey, src.ProductName, GETDATE() AS LastSeenDate FROM silver.UpdateJoinTarget AS tgt INNER JOIN import_bronze_product AS src ON tgt.ProductAlternateKey = CAST(src.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) ), final AS ( SELECT ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName, LastSeenDate FROM logical_update_join ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both queries read from bronze.Product and silver.UpdateJoinTarget via INNER JOIN. No filters restrict rows before the join.

output_columns.summary: Both produce three columns: ProductAlternateKey (NVARCHAR(25)), EnglishProductName (NVARCHAR(50)), LastSeenDate (DATETIME). Refactored aliases ProductName as EnglishProductName in the final CTE.

joins.summary: Both use INNER JOIN on tgt.ProductAlternateKey = CAST(src.ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)). Join condition is logically equivalent.

filters.summary: No WHERE clauses in either query. Both return all matched join rows.

aggregation_grain.summary: No aggregation or grouping in either query. Both are row-by-row transformations with no grain reduction.
